meta.method: Five Whys per defect class; each root cause tied to observed in-repo evidence. No speculation beyond evidence.

defect_classes.class: Status staleness (CSV Backlog while shipped on main)
defect_classes.evidence: DOC-015 (#612) and IFC-211 (#597) shipped to origin/main but CSV still reads Backlog; 8 of the 11 rows the 2026-07-21 backlog-truth-audit flagged were already flipped, 2 more shipped after it ran.
five_whys: Why is the CSV stale? Because status is flipped manually after merge, in a separate step from the merge.
Why is it a separate step? Because merge (GitHub) and the source-of-truth CSV (repo file) are not linked by automation.
Why no link? Because there is no post-merge hook that reconciles a task id in a PR title to its CSV Status.
Why not? Because task tracking was retrofitted onto an existing PR flow; the CSV was treated as human-owned.
Root cause: No automated PR-merge -> CSV-status reconciliation; the single source of truth depends on manual, easily-skipped edits.
defect_classes.root_cause: No automated merge->status reconciliation; CSV status is a manual after-thought decoupled from the merge event.

defect_classes.class: Missing / unverifiable completion evidence (absent or unmeasured timings)
defect_classes.evidence: Committed-verifiable from task-tracking.json: 34 of 62 completed tasks record completed_at:null and 5 have no tracking file at all -- ~39 completed tasks carry no real committed completion timestamp. Of the 28 populated timestamps only 5 form x2 batch-session pairs (no larger cluster). (A separate GITIGNORED generated cache showed larger bulk-sync duplicate clusters e.g. 2026-06-27T23:53:49.694Z, but that cache is not committed and not reproducible in a clean checkout, so it is not treated as committed evidence.)
five_whys: Why placeholder timestamps? Because completion time is written by a bulk sync, not captured at the moment work finished.
Why bulk sync? Because many tasks were marked Completed en-masse rather than through /exec, which stamps real timings.
Why en-masse? Because delivery outpaced governance; rows were reconciled in batches after the fact.
Why did governance lag? Because attestation is a manual /exec Phase-5 step with no gate blocking a Completed flip without it.
Root cause: "Completed" can be set without an attestation or a real timing capture; the tracker accepts unverified completion.
defect_classes.root_cause: No enforcement that a Completed status carries a real, per-task attestation with measured timings; bulk sync can mint completion.

defect_classes.class: Ambiguous / unenforced Definition of Done (attestation gap)
defect_classes.evidence: 18 Completed tasks have no canonical attestation.json (13 PG module-settings + 5 INFRA-TF). 9 attested tasks carry an honest KPI gap (e.g. IFC-310 real-DB bench null; Lighthouse >=90 asserted but not CI-proven).
five_whys: Why no attestation on 18 done tasks? Because they shipped via PR/batch without running /exec attestation.
Why skip attestation? Because DoD ("Lighthouse >=90", ">=90% coverage", "verified by: pnpm test") is not machine-checked at merge.
Why not machine-checked? Because the gate that would check it (pre-ship/CI) does not assert attestation existence per task id.
Why not? Because attestation lives in .specify and is decoupled from the PR that delivers the code.
Root cause: DoD is prose, not an executable contract wired into the merge gate; attestation is optional in practice.
defect_classes.root_cause: DoD is narrative and attestation is not a merge-blocking, per-task gate — so "done" is declared, not proven.

defect_classes.evidence: All four DORA metrics resolve to UNKNOWN: no deployment log, no deploy<->incident linkage in-repo. INFRA-TF-004 monitoring module and IFC-032 OTel wiring are themselves Sprint-18 items (delivered as code, but observability endpoints not confirmed live).
five_whys: Why is DORA UNKNOWN? Because deployments and incidents are not recorded where the audit can read them.
Why not recorded? Because deploy events (Railway/Vercel) and incidents (ops) live outside the repo with no exported log.
Why no export? Because observability-as-code (monitoring module, OTel) was built this sprint but not verified live end-to-end.
Why unverified? Because "provisioned in Terraform" was accepted as done without a live-endpoint check.
Root cause: Delivery of telemetry infrastructure was not gated on a live-signal proof, so the org still cannot measure flow.
defect_classes.root_cause: Telemetry was delivered as code but never verified as a live signal; DORA remains unmeasurable by construction.

defect_classes.evidence: sprint_start_date/end_date are null; 13 tasks injected mid-window (2026-04-19); window spans ~149 days vs 14 nominal; SPI 0.36.
five_whys: Why did the sprint span ~149 days? Because it had no end date and scope kept being added.
Why added continuously? Because new work (module-settings batch) was filed straight into the active sprint as Backlog.
Why into the active sprint? Because there is no "sprint is closed to new scope" control.
Why no control? Because the tracker treats a sprint as a label, not a time-boxed commitment with a locked backlog.
Root cause: Sprint is a bucket/label, not a time-boxed commitment; without start/end + backlog-lock, scope is unbounded.
defect_classes.root_cause: Sprint modelled as an open label, not a time-boxed commitment with locked scope and dates.
